title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (July 15)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: Abington's most expensive sale was a one-family colonial on Crabtree Lane for $1,380,000 with 4,232 square feet.
Acton's highest sale involved a colonial on Woodchester Drive, selling for $2,170,000 and measuring 5,081 square feet.
In Amesbury, an 1850-built colonial at Main St. sold at $2,450,000, featuring 8 rooms and 4 bedrooms.

title: Active-duty Everett firefighter dies from occupational cancer after 30 years of service, department says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Everett firefighter John Benoit, 63, died from occupational cancer after over 30 years in the department, officials announced.
Benoit, who served in the Air Force, continued a family legacy in firefighting after joining the Everett Fire Department in 1996.
Benoit is survived by his wife, two children, and extended family; his wake is scheduled for Wednesday in Everett.

title: 2 Dorchester women charged in alleged human trafficking scheme
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Yuberkys Recci and Keysha Ortiz from Dorchester were arrested for alleged human trafficking involving a homeless woman.
The victim was coerced into commercial sex work and held captive across Massachusetts and New Hampshire, authorities report.
Recci and Ortiz face charges including kidnapping, trafficking, weapons offenses, and drug distribution, per Suffolk County DA.

title: ‘Brilliant, unique soul’: Mother remembers Fall River teen who drowned in pond
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Tristan Major, also known as Rohan Rhys, drowned in South Watuppa Pond, Fall River on July 12.
Tristan, remembered as a "brilliant, unique soul," aimed to start a therapeutic horse ranch with his family.
Police divers recovered Tristan's body after a two-hour search; no foul play is suspected in the drowning.

title: Jennifer Garner posts throwback video of karaoke night at Nantucket restaurant
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Jennifer Garner shared a throwback video of her and 'The Five-Star Weekend' co-stars singing karaoke in Nantucket.
The video, posted on Monday, features Garner singing ABBA's 'Dancing Queen' at Gaslight, a venue in downtown Nantucket.
Local guide Bill Jamieson, known for his Nantucket Ghost Walk tours, joined the actresses onstage during the performance.

title: Here’s what meteorologists are saying about the yellow skies across the region
author: Jack Pickell
bullets: Wildfire smoke from Canada causes yellow skies over Boston, limiting expected heat due to combined cloud cover.
Meteorologists predict another wave of smoke with potential surface level impact, affecting temperatures on Wednesday.
Forecasters highlight ongoing smoky conditions giving skies a yellowish glow; expected to continue into Thursday.

title: A very special traffic cone arrives in Boston, courtesy of our new friends in Glasgow
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A signed traffic cone from Glasgow arrived in Boston to mark a new partnership between the two cities.
The cone will be used to promote mental health fundraising for organizations in both Scotland and Massachusetts.
Boston and Glasgow announced plans to become sister cities, strengthening ties formed during the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

title: 3 more MBTA employees charged with overtime fraud, falsifying track inspections
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Three MBTA employees charged with overtime fraud and falsifying Red Line track inspection reports at Cabot Yard.
Defendants allegedly worked on personal vehicles and falsified timesheets to claim overtime they didn't work.
All seven charged, including a supervisor, have pleaded not guilty to the federal conspiracy charges.

title: The World Cup was great, but Boston still doesn’t need the Olympics
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France won the last World Cup quarterfinal match held in Foxborough, concluding Boston's hosting of seven games in 2026.
Hosting the World Cup cost Massachusetts taxpayers nearly $100 million, mostly for public transportation improvements in Foxborough.
Boston's previous Olympic bid faced criticism for underestimated costs, and required extensive construction of new sports venues.

title: Bill Belichick’s daughter-in-law wanted for arrest in North Carolina after traffic stop
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jen Belichick, married to Bill Belichick's son Steve, is wanted in North Carolina after missing a court appearance.
She was cited for speeding at 88 mph in a 70 mph zone in Brunswick County.
Bill Belichick leads UNC football, with a $50 million contract since 2024, after leaving the Patriots.
